language.title: Language
language.sync_failed: Language saved on this device, but failed to sync to your account. Other devices may show the previous language.

notifications.title: Inbox Notifications
notifications.description: Control which events generate inbox notifications. Muted event types are silently filtered — you can still see them by visiting the issue directly.

assignments.label: Assignments
assignments.description: When you are assigned or unassigned from an issue

status_changes.label: Status changes
status_changes.description: When an issue you follow changes status (e.g. todo, in progress, done)

comments.label: Comments & Mentions
comments.description: New comments on issues you follow, or when someone @mentions you

updates.label: Priority & Due date
updates.description: When priority or due date changes on issues you follow

agent_activity.label: Agent activity
agent_activity.description: When an agent task completes or fails

system.title: System Notifications
system.description: Control native OS notification banners shown when Multica is in the background.
system.label: Show system notifications
system.hint: Show a banner from your operating system for new inbox items when the app isn't focused.

tokens.title: API Tokens
tokens.description: Personal access tokens allow the CLI and external integrations to authenticate with your account.

revoke_dialog.title: Revoke token
revoke_dialog.description: This token will be permanently revoked and can no longer be used. This cannot be undone.

created_dialog.title: Token created
created_dialog.description: Copy your personal access token now. You won't be able to see it again.

workspace.description_placeholder: What does this workspace focus on?
workspace.context_placeholder: Background information and context for AI agents working in this workspace
workspace.orchestrator_hint: Optional. When set, this agent gets woken on every agent-authored issue comment to coordinate the workflow — acknowledge completion, reassign, change status, or notify a human. Leave as "No orchestrator" to keep the current behavior (only the assigned agent is woken).
workspace.manage_hint: Only admins and owners can update workspace settings.
workspace.leave_sole_owner: You're the only owner. Promote another member to owner first, or delete the workspace.
workspace.leave_sole_member: You're the only member. Delete the workspace to leave.
workspace.leave_confirm_description: Leave {{name}}? You will lose access until re-invited.
workspace.delete_description: Permanently delete this workspace and its data.

channels.enable_description: Multi-participant chat alongside the issue board, with public channels, private channels, and direct messages. When off, the sidebar entry hides and Channels endpoints return 404.
channels.retention_description: Messages older than this are hidden from view. Default: 90 days. Per-channel overrides take precedence.
channels.retention_invalid: Retention must be between 1 and 3650 days.
channels.retention_warning: Heads up: existing messages older than {{days}} days will be hidden after the next cleanup run.

delete_workspace_dialog.title: Delete workspace
delete_workspace_dialog.description: This cannot be undone. All issues, agents, and data will be permanently removed.

repositories.description: Git repositories associated with this workspace. Agents use these to clone and work on code.
repositories.manage_hint: Only admins and owners can manage repositories.

members.revoke_invitation_description: Revoke the invitation to {{email}}? They will no longer be able to join this workspace.
members.remove_member_description: Remove {{name}} from {{workspace}}? They will lose access to this workspace.
members.cannot_demote_last_owner_title: Promote another member to owner first — a workspace must keep at least one owner.

connection.title: Connection details
connection.description: Paste these into the CLI, the MCP server, or any other client. Pair with a personal access token from above.

mcp.title: Model Context Protocol
mcp.description: The Multica binary ships an MCP server (`multica mcp-stdio`). Configure your AI client to launch it with the env vars below — no separate install needed.
mcp.claude_desktop_description: Add to ~/Library/Application Support/Claude/claude_desktop_config.json (macOS) and restart the app.
mcp.security_note: Security: the MCP server runs locally and acts on behalf of whoever owns the token. Treat it like a password and revoke if it leaks. Mutating tools (issue create/update, channel post, autopilot trigger) all run with your account's permissions — there is no separate MCP-only scope.
mcp.step_create_token_body: Use the section above. Name it something memorable like 'MCP server' so you can revoke it later if needed.
mcp.step_try_body: Start a new session in your AI client and prompt with something like 'List my open Multica issues' — the assistant should call multica_issue_list and return your issue list. From there it can create issues, assign agents, post in channels, trigger autopilots — anything the tool list covers.
mcp.step_register_other_clients_body: Any client that speaks the standard MCP stdio transport works. The command is `multica mcp-stdio` plus the three env vars from the snippet above.

rest.title: REST API
rest.intro: Everything the web app, CLI, and MCP server do is available over plain HTTPS. Endpoints follow REST conventions: list / get / create / update / delete on resource paths, JSON in and out, bearer-token auth.
rest.auth_body_prefix: Send your personal access token as a
rest.auth_body_bearer_suffix: header. Workspace-scoped endpoints also need an
rest.auth_body_workspace_suffix: header — without it the API can't resolve which workspace the call should target. Write requests use
rest.endpoints_body_prefix: Paths are relative to the API base URL above. Path parameters are
rest.endpoints_body_suffix: ; everything else is a literal segment. Where a path has two methods listed, both are supported on the same URL.
rest.conventions_body_offset_suffix: query params (default 50, max 200). Channel and chat timelines paginate by
rest.conventions_body_before_suffix: (RFC3339 timestamp, exclusive) instead of offset because rapid inserts at the head would otherwise cause skips. Errors return
rest.conventions_body_error_suffix: with the appropriate 4xx / 5xx status. Empty responses use 204 (no body).
rest.no_openapi_body_prefix: the canonical reference is the route table at
rest.no_openapi_body_suffix: in the source tree. The list above covers what the web app, CLI, and MCP server use day-to-day.
